name: Hulmes Transportation Svc LTD
fleet_size_est: unknown (van fleet, ~1200-series numbering used on PVTA routes pre-2018)
employees_est: ~210 (per source list; likely overstated given current scope)
districts_or_customers: Western MA seniors and disabled riders
Former PVTA fixed-route van subcontractor (Ware Shuttle, Palmer Shuttle, Nashawannuck Express) until 2018
recent_news: Lost PVTA van subcontract to National Express in summer 2018; pivoted toward senior/disability door-to-door transport. Actively hiring drivers as of 2024-2025.
fit_notes: Marginal fit as a school-bus tuck-in: business is primarily senior/ADA/paratransit, not yellow school bus. Could be of interest if Summit wants to expand into adjacent special-needs/NEMT verticals in Western MA, but does not match core student-transport thesis. Lost largest transit contract in 2018.

name: Logan Bus Co Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (Logan family; Logan Bus Co. & Affiliates includes Little Richie Bus Service, Little Linda Bus Company, Logan Transportation Systems)
fleet_size_est: ~2,500 routes across affiliates (one of the largest NYC private school bus operators)
service_types: school (NYC DOE general-ed and special-needs routes)
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education (largest NYC private contractor)
Facilities in Queens, Bronx, Brooklyn, Nassau County
recent_news: Awarded ~$206M NYC DOE contract in 2020 despite past bribery-of-city-officials admission. First EV school bus on NYC streets and first NY operator to pilot stop-arm cameras. EVP Corey Muirhead named SBF Trailblazer 2023, NYSBCA past president. Actively negotiated 2023 NYC DOE contract extension averting strike.
fit_notes: Far too large for a typical Summit tuck-in (likely $150M+ revenue, dominant NYC player). Not a tuck-in candidate — would be a platform-scale or transformative acquisition. Strong NYC DOE entrenchment plus past bribery-disclosure history makes it complex. Low succession risk given young, prominent EVP. Better treated as a long-shot strategic peer than a tuck-in.

name: Northstar Bus Lines LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (Regan family); operates affiliated brand American Student Transportation
fleet_size_est: ~80-120 buses across Maple Grove and Blaine terminals (includes 3 electric school buses from Osseo pilot)
districts_or_customers: Maple Grove / North Metro / Blaine area churches and community charters
recent_news: Participated in MN electric school bus pilot with Osseo School District; Matt Regan installed 5 EV charging stations at Maple Grove garage. 2020 Traffic Safety Innovation Award; 2022 Best of Blaine. Brand affiliated with American Student Transportation (Blaine).
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: ~$10M revenue, family-owned, third generation, multiple Twin Cities suburban district contracts, EV-forward (de-risks future contract bids), two terminals provide route density. Matt Regan still relatively young so succession is medium/low, but Summit could position as a growth partner. Note: distinct from the unrelated Patrick O. Regan/Minnesota Coaches group recently sold to Tiger Infrastructure Partners — diligence should confirm no family/operational ties.

name: Voigt's Bus
districts_or_customers: Anoka-Hennepin School District (largest district in MN) — multiple routes
Multi-region MN charter and event clients
recent_news: Troy Voigt (then-president) passed away in 2022; leadership transitioned to brother Lance Voigt and sister Lea McStott with niece Voigt-Ely joining as 4th-gen stockholder. Member of International Motorcoach Group.
fit_notes: Attractive Minnesota tuck-in: 75+ year-old multi-generational family business with diversified school/charter/motorcoach mix, 4 facilities (Cold Spring, Minneapolis, St. Augusta, St. Cloud), anchor contract with Anoka-Hennepin (MN's largest district). Recent generational transition after Troy Voigt's 2022 death raises real succession-signal — 4th gen now actively involved but ownership group is small and could be open to exit. Diversified revenue (charter + motorcoach + body shop) is both a positive (revenue mix) and a caveat (Summit core thesis is yellow bus).

name: Marschall Line
owner_or_parent: Tiger Infrastructure Partners Fund IV (via TIP Minnesota Coaches Acquisition LLC / MNC Holdings, LP); operates under Minnesota Coaches umbrella alongside Mid-County Bus Company, Rehbein Transit, Ready Bus, Voyageur Bus, Minn-Dakota Coaches, Faribault Transportation
principals: Patrick O. Regan (prior majority owner / Minnesota Coaches president, retained minority/operating role)
fleet_size_est: ~110 power units (Marschall Line / Mid-County Bus combined)
employees_est: ~118 drivers (Marschall Line); ~200 total per source list
recent_news: January 2025: Surface Transportation Board tentatively approved Tiger Infrastructure Partners' acquisition of control of MN Coaches platform (Marschall Line + 6 affiliated carriers) from Patrick O. Regan. Comments deadline February 24, 2025. Transaction expected to fund next chapter of growth.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate — Marschall Line is now part of Tiger Infrastructure Partners' Minnesota Coaches roll-up platform (closed early 2025). Tiger is a competing PE infrastructure consolidator in the same MN school-bus market that Summit (I Squared) would target. Worth tracking as a potential competitor on future MN deals or, conceivably, as a future portfolio swap. Confirms PE consolidation thesis is active in Minnesota.
